--- name: neuman-brain-ux-designer description: Professional UI/UX design expertise for Neuman Brain including design thinking, user psychology (Hick's/Fitts's/Jakob's Law), visual hierarchy, interaction patterns, accessibility beyond WCAG, performance-driven design, and design critique framework. Use when designing features, improving UX, solving user problems, creating interaction patterns, optimizing perceived performance, or conducting design reviews. --- # Neuman Brain UX Designer - Professional Design Expertise **Purpose:** Apply professional UI/UX design thinking to improve and evolve Neuman Brain's user experience. This skill teaches Claude to think like an expert product designer, going beyond style guides to solve real user problems. **Builds on:** `frontend-design@anthropics/claude-code` plugin + Neuman Brain design system constraints --- ## 🎯 Design Philosophy **You are a senior product designer with expertise in:** - User-centered design and design thinking - Visual design principles and design systems - Interaction design and micro-interactions - Accessibility and inclusive design - Performance-driven design - User psychology and behavioral design **Your Role:** 1. **Critique** - Evaluate existing designs against professional standards 2. **Improve** - Suggest specific, actionable improvements 3. **Innovate** - Propose new design solutions based on UX best practices 4. **Educate** - Explain the "why" behind design decisions **Constraints (Neuman Brain-specific):** - Brand colors: Magenta (#E91E8C), Cyan (#00C9FF), Purple gradients - Aesthetic: Retro-futurism, cyberpunk professional - Performance: < 1s load, < 100ms interactions, 60fps animations - Privacy-first: Local-first, no tracking, user control - Bundle size: < 200KB gzipped *Reference: `.claude/skills/neuman-brain-design-system/` for current implementation* --- ## 🧠 Design Thinking Framework ### 1. Understand the User Problem **Before designing, ask:** - What problem are we solving for users? - What's the user's context (device, environment, emotional state)? - What's the user's goal and motivation? - What are the pain points in the current experience? **Example:** ``` User wants to: Create a new task quickly Current experience: Click "+" → Fill form → Click "Save" (3 steps, 5 clicks) Pain points: Too many steps, form feels heavy, no keyboard shortcuts Design solution: Inline quick-add input with Enter to save, Tab to add tags, keyboard-first ``` ### 2. Apply Jobs-to-be-Done Framework **Every feature has a "job" the user is hiring it to do:** ``` When I [situation] I want to [motivation] So I can [expected outcome] ``` **Example:** ``` When I'm in a meeting taking notes I want to quickly capture action items without leaving the note So I can stay focused on the conversation, not the tool ``` **Design Implications:** - Inline task creation from notes (no modal disruption) - Voice-to-text support (hands-free capture) - Auto-link tasks to current note (reduce manual work) ### 3. Progressive Disclosure **Show only what's necessary, when it's necessary.** **Levels of Information:** 1. **Primary** - Always visible (core functionality) 2. **Secondary** - Visible on hover/focus (supporting actions) 3. **Tertiary** - Hidden in menus/modals (advanced features) **Example - Task Card:** ``` Primary: Task title, status, due date Secondary: Edit, delete, tags (on hover) Tertiary: History, comments, attachments (in modal) ``` ### 4. Reduce Cognitive Load **Working Memory Limit: 7±2 items (Miller's Law)** **Strategies:** - **Chunking** - Group related items (max 7 per group) - **Recognition over Recall** - Show options, don't require memorization - **Defaults** - Pre-fill with smart defaults - **Consistency** - Same patterns across the app **Example - Long Form:** ``` ❌ BAD: Single page with 20 fields ✅ GOOD: 4-step wizard with 5 fields each + progress indicator ``` --- ## 🎨 Visual Design Principles ### 1. Visual Hierarchy (Importance → Size/Weight/Color) **Typographic Scale:** ``` H1: 36-48px - Page titles (most important) H2: 24-30px - Section titles H3: 20-24px - Subsection titles Body: 16px - Default (optimal readability) Small: 14px - Captions, metadata Tiny: 12px - Timestamps, labels (use sparingly) ``` **Weight Hierarchy:** ``` Bold (700) - Primary actions, active states Semibold (600) - Headings, important text Medium (500) - Subheadings Regular (400) - Body text (default) Light (300) - Deemphasized text (use sparingly) ``` **Color Hierarchy:** ``` High contrast - Primary content (black/white) Medium contrast - Secondary content (gray-700/gray-300) Low contrast - Tertiary content (gray-500) Accent - Interactive elements (magenta, cyan) ``` **Example - Task Card:** ```tsx
{/* PRIMARY - Task title (bold, high contrast) */}

Implement dark mode toggle

{/* SECONDARY - Metadata (medium weight, medium contrast) */}

Due: Nov 15, 2025

{/* TERTIARY - Timestamp (regular weight, low contrast) */}

Created 2 hours ago

``` ### 2. Gestalt Principles (How Users Perceive Groups) **Proximity** - Items close together are perceived as related ```tsx ❌ BAD: Equal spacing between all items

Settings

Advanced

✅ GOOD: Group related items with proximity

Settings

Advanced

``` **Similarity** - Similar items are perceived as related ```tsx ✅ Use consistent styling for related elements
{/* All action buttons same style */} {/* Destructive action different style */}
``` **Closure** - Users mentally complete incomplete shapes ```tsx ✅ Use partial borders for elegance
Important note
``` **Figure-Ground** - Separate foreground from background ```tsx ✅ Use elevation and contrast
{/* Background (blurred overlay) */}
{/* Foreground (modal pops out) */}
Modal content
``` **Common Region** - Items within boundaries are grouped ```tsx ✅ Use cards/containers to group
{/* All items within this card are related */}

Account Settings

``` ### 3. Contrast & Accessibility **WCAG 2.2 AA Requirements:** - Normal text: 4.5:1 contrast minimum - Large text (18pt+): 3:1 contrast minimum - UI components: 3:1 contrast minimum **Contrast Ratios (Pre-calculated for Neuman Brain):** ```css /* ✅ PASS AA & AAA */ #000000 (black) on #FFFFFF (white) = 21:1 #E91E8C (magenta) on #FFFFFF (white) = 6.2:1 #00C9FF (cyan) on #000000 (black) = 8.1:1 #FFFFFF (white) on #E91E8C (magenta) = 4.7:1 /* ⚠️ PASS AA (but not AAA) */ #64748B (slate-500) on #FFFFFF (white) = 4.6:1 /* ❌ FAIL - Do not use */ #FF7EC7 (magenta-300) on #FFFFFF = 2.1:1 #7FE4FF (cyan-300) on #FFFFFF = 1.8:1 ``` **When to Use High Contrast:** - Body text (always) - Critical UI elements (buttons, inputs) - Error messages - Focus indicators **When Lower Contrast is OK:** - Disabled states - Placeholder text - Tertiary metadata (timestamps) ### 4. White Space (Negative Space) **"White space is not wasted space - it's breathing room for the eye."** **Spacing Scale (Neuman Brain - 4px base):** ``` 4px (space-1) - Tight spacing (icon + text) 8px (space-2) - Compact elements 12px (space-3) - Form field spacing 16px (space-4) - Component padding 20px (space-5) - Section spacing 24px (space-6) - Card padding 32px (space-8) - Large gaps 48px (space-12) - Section dividers ``` **Macro White Space (Between Sections):** ```tsx ✅ GOOD: Generous spacing between major sections
Content 1
Content 2
Content 3
``` **Micro White Space (Within Components):** ```tsx ✅ GOOD: Tight spacing within related items
Label
``` ### 5. Alignment & Grid Systems **Types of Alignment:** - **Edge** - Align to left/right edges (most common) - **Center** - Align to center (use for symmetry) - **Baseline** - Align text baselines (mixed font sizes) **Grid System:** ```tsx ✅ Use consistent grid (12-column or flex gap)
``` --- ## 🎭 Interaction Design ### 1. Affordances (Visual Hints for Interaction) **Buttons should look clickable:** ```tsx ✅ GOOD: Clear affordance ❌ BAD: No affordance
Click Me
``` **Interactive elements:** - Cursor changes to `pointer` on hover - Visual feedback on hover (color shift, lift, glow) - Active state on click (pressed effect) - Disabled state is visually distinct (opacity, grayscale) ### 2. Feedback (Immediate Response to User Actions) **Types of Feedback:** **Visual Feedback:** ```tsx // Button press effect // Loading state // Success state ``` **Haptic Feedback (where supported):** ```tsx const handleClick = () => { // Trigger haptic feedback on mobile if ('vibrate' in navigator) { navigator.vibrate(10); // 10ms subtle vibration } // ... handle click }; ``` **Toast Notifications:** ```tsx // Success toast (auto-dismiss) toast.success('Task created successfully', { duration: 3000, position: 'bottom-right', }); // Error toast (persistent, user dismisses) toast.error('Failed to save. Check your connection.', { duration: Infinity, action: { label: 'Retry', onClick: retry }, }); ``` **Timing Rules:** - Instant feedback: < 100ms (button press, hover) - Expected wait: 1-3s (loading spinner) - Unexpected wait: > 3s (progress bar with percentage) ### 3. State Transitions (Smooth Changes) **Animation Principles (Disney's 12 Principles Applied to UI):** **Ease In/Out:** ```css /* Smooth acceleration/deceleration */ transition: all 300ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); ``` **Follow Through:** ```tsx // Modal opens → overlay fades in → content slides up Modal content ``` **Anticipation:** ```tsx // Button shrinks slightly before action (like a spring) Submit ``` ### 4. Micro-Interactions (Delightful Details) **Examples:** **Checkbox Animation:** ```tsx ``` **Empty State with Personality:** ```tsx

No tasks yet

Create your first task to get started

``` **Loading Skeleton (Better than spinners):** ```tsx
``` --- ## 🧪 User Psychology & Behavioral Design ### 1. Hick's Law (Choices → Decision Time) **"Decision time increases logarithmically with the number of choices."** **Implications:** ```tsx ❌ BAD: 15 buttons in a toolbar {/* ... 8 more buttons */} ✅ GOOD: Group into 3-5 primary actions + overflow menu Export Import Print {/* ... */} ``` ### 2. Fitts's Law (Size & Distance → Interaction Time) **"Time to reach a target = function of distance and size."** **Implications:** **Touch Targets (Mobile):** ``` Minimum: 44x44px (Apple HIG, WCAG 2.2) Recommended: 48x48px (Material Design) Optimal: 56x56px (easy one-handed use) ``` **Proximity (Place related actions close):** ```tsx ✅ GOOD: Action buttons near content {/* Actions right here, not far away */}
``` ### 3. Jakob's Law (Familiarity → Usability) **"Users spend most of their time on OTHER sites. They expect yours to work the same way."** **Follow Conventions:** - Logo in top-left (links to home) - Search in top-right - Hamburger menu for mobile navigation - Double-click to edit inline - Ctrl+S to save, Ctrl+Z to undo - Swipe to delete (mobile) **Don't Reinvent:** ```tsx ✅ Standard date picker (users know how to use it) ❌ Custom date picker with unusual interaction {/* Users need to learn new pattern */} ``` ### 4. Zeigarnik Effect (Incomplete Tasks → Mental Tension) **"People remember uncompleted tasks better than completed ones."** **Implications:** **Progress Indicators:** ```tsx
Profile Setup 3 of 5 completed
``` **Show What's Left:** ```tsx ✓ Add profile photo ✓ Set display name ✓ Choose theme ⭘ Connect calendar (optional) ⭘ Invite team members (optional) ``` ### 5. Peak-End Rule (Users Remember Peaks & Endings) **"Overall experience is judged by peaks (best/worst moments) and endings."** **Design Implications:** **Create Peaks (Delightful Moments):** ```tsx // Confetti on task completion const handleComplete = () => { completeTask(taskId); confetti({ particleCount: 100, spread: 70, origin: { y: 0.6 }, }); }; ``` **End on High Note:** ```tsx // Success screen after onboarding

You're all set!

Your workspace is ready to go.

``` --- ## ♿ Accessibility & Inclusive Design ### 1. Beyond Compliance (WCAG 2.2 AA is the Floor, Not the Ceiling) **Inclusive Design Principles:** 1. **Equitable** - Everyone gets equal access 2. **Flexible** - Works for diverse abilities 3. **Simple** - Easy to understand 4. **Perceptible** - Information is easily perceived 5. **Fault-tolerant** - Minimizes errors and consequences ### 2. Keyboard Navigation (Power Users & Accessibility) **Navigation Patterns:** ``` Tab - Move forward through interactive elements Shift+Tab - Move backward Enter/Space - Activate buttons, toggles Escape - Close modals, cancel actions Arrow keys - Navigate lists, menus, sliders Home/End - Jump to start/end ``` **Implementation:** ```tsx // Custom keyboard shortcuts useEffect(() => { const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => { // Cmd+K or Ctrl+K - Open command palette if ((e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey) && e.key === 'k') { e.preventDefault(); openCommandPalette(); } // Cmd+N - New task if ((e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey) && e.key === 'n') { e.preventDefault(); createTask(); } }; window.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown); return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown); }, []); ``` **Visual Keyboard Shortcut Hints:** ```tsx ``` ### 3. Screen Reader Support (Semantic HTML + ARIA) **Semantic HTML First:** ```tsx ✅ GOOD: Use semantic elements
  • Dashboard
  • Tasks
❌ BAD: Generic divs
Dashboard
Tasks
``` **ARIA When Necessary:** ```tsx // Live regions for dynamic updates
{taskCount} tasks remaining
// Progress indicators
{progress}%
// Modal dialogs

Confirm Delete

This action cannot be undone.

``` ### 4. Motion & Animation Accessibility **Respect `prefers-reduced-motion`:** ```css @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; } } ``` **React Implementation:** ```tsx const prefersReducedMotion = window.matchMedia( '(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)' ).matches; Content ``` ### 5. Color Blindness Considerations **Don't Rely on Color Alone:** ```tsx ❌ BAD: Color only
Error
Success
✅ GOOD: Color + icon + text
Error: Failed to save
Success: Saved successfully
``` **Color Blind Safe Palettes:** ``` Neuman Brain palette is color-blind safe: - Magenta (#E91E8C) - Distinct hue - Cyan (#00C9FF) - Distinct hue - Purple (#9333EA) - Bridge color Test with: https://www.color-blindness.com/coblis-color-blindness-simulator/ ``` --- ## 🚀 Performance-Driven Design ### 1. Perceived Performance (Feels Fast ≠ Is Fast) **Optimistic UI Updates:** ```tsx const handleLike = async () => { // Update UI immediately (optimistic) setLiked(true); setLikeCount(prev => prev + 1); try { // Then sync with server await api.likePost(postId); } catch (error) { // Rollback on error setLiked(false); setLikeCount(prev => prev - 1); toast.error('Failed to like post'); } }; ``` **Skeleton Screens (Better than Spinners):** ```tsx {loading ? (
) : ( )} ``` **Progress Indication:** ```tsx // Bad: Indeterminate spinner (feels slow) // Good: Percentage (feels faster, even if same duration)
{progress}%

Uploading...

``` ### 2. Design for Performance Budgets **Neuman Brain Constraints:** - Bundle: < 200KB gzipped - Images: < 100KB per image - Total page weight: < 500KB **Design Decisions:** **Use SVG Icons (Scalable, Tiny):** ```tsx ✅ GOOD: SVG icons (2KB for entire icon set) import { Plus, Edit, Trash } from 'lucide-react'; ❌ BAD: Icon fonts (50KB+ for full set) ``` **Lazy Load Images:** ```tsx ``` **Prioritize Critical CSS:** ```tsx // Inline critical CSS in // Load full CSS async ``` ### 3. Animation Performance (60fps Non-Negotiable) **GPU-Accelerated Properties Only:** ```css /* ✅ FAST - GPU accelerated */ transform: translateY(-4px); opacity: 0.5; filter: blur(10px); /* ❌ SLOW - Triggers layout/paint */ margin-top: -4px; width: 100px; background-color: red; ``` **Force GPU Acceleration:** ```css .animated { transform: translateZ(0); /* Force GPU layer */ will-change: transform, opacity; /* Hint to browser (use sparingly) */ } ``` **Remove `will-change` After Animation:** ```tsx const [isHovered, setIsHovered] = useState(false);
setIsHovered(true)} onMouseLeave={() => setIsHovered(false)} style={{ willChange: isHovered ? 'transform' : 'auto' }} > Content
``` --- ## 🎨 Design Critique Framework **Use this framework to evaluate any design (current or proposed).** ### 1. Usability Heuristics (Nielsen's 10) 1. **Visibility of System Status** - Does the user always know what's happening? - Loading states, progress bars, confirmations 2. **Match Between System & Real World** - Does it use familiar language/concepts? - "Archive" not "Soft delete", "Folder" not "Container" 3. **User Control & Freedom** - Can users undo mistakes easily? - Undo toast, confirm dialogs for destructive actions 4. **Consistency & Standards** - Are patterns consistent across the app? - Same button styles, same spacing, same terminology 5. **Error Prevention** - Does the design prevent errors? - Disable buttons when invalid, validate on blur, confirm before delete 6. **Recognition Over Recall** - Are options visible? - Dropdown menus, autocomplete, recent items 7. **Flexibility & Efficiency** - Can power users work faster? - Keyboard shortcuts, bulk actions, quick-add 8. **Aesthetic & Minimalist** - Is every element necessary? - Remove decorative elements, focus on content 9. **Error Recovery** - Are error messages helpful? - Specific problem, actionable solution, retry button 10. **Help & Documentation** - Is help contextual and searchable? - Tooltips, inline help, searchable docs ### 2. Critique Template **When evaluating a design, ask:** ```markdown ## Design Critique: [Component/Feature Name] ### 1. First Impression (5-second test) - What stands out immediately? - Is the primary action obvious? - Does it feel too cluttered or too empty? ### 2. Visual Hierarchy - Is the most important element the most prominent? - Can you scan the page in an F or Z pattern? - Are related items grouped visually? ### 3. Usability - Can a new user complete the primary task without help? - Are there any confusing elements? - Does it follow familiar patterns (Jakob's Law)? ### 4. Accessibility - Keyboard navigation works? - Color contrast passes WCAG AA? - Screen reader friendly? ### 5. Performance - Any layout-triggering animations? - Heavy images or assets? - Smooth 60fps interactions? ### 6. Recommendations - [Specific, actionable improvements] - [Priority: High/Medium/Low] ``` ### 3. Before/After Pattern **Always present improvements as Before → After comparisons:** ```markdown ### Current Implementation (Before) **Issues:** 1. Primary button is same size as secondary (no hierarchy) 2. Delete button too close to Save (accidental clicks) 3. No loading state (feels unresponsive) ### Proposed Improvement (After) **Changes:** 1. Increase primary button size 40px → 48px (more prominent) 2. Move delete to overflow menu (reduce error risk) 3. Add spinner + disabled state while saving (feedback) **Expected Impact:** - 50% fewer accidental deletes (moved to menu) - Clearer primary action (size hierarchy) - Better perceived performance (immediate feedback) ``` --- ## 📚 Design Resources & References ### Professional Frameworks **Design Systems:** - Material Design (Google) - https://m3.material.io/ - Human Interface Guidelines (Apple) - https://developer.apple.com/design/ - Fluent Design (Microsoft) - https://fluent2.microsoft.design/ **UX Laws:** - Laws of UX - https://lawsofux.com/ - Jakob Nielsen's Usability Heuristics - Don Norman's Design of Everyday Things principles **Accessibility:** - WCAG 2.2 Guidelines - https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/quickref/ - Inclusive Design Toolkit (Microsoft) - WebAIM Contrast Checker ### Neuman Brain Design System **Internal References:** - `.claude/skills/neuman-brain-design-system/` - Brand colors, components, patterns - `docs/brand/brand_guide.md` - Brand narrative, voice, visual identity - `docs/technical/development_principles.md` - Performance, accessibility, privacy **Code References:** - `config/tailwind.config.js` - Design tokens (colors, spacing, typography) - `src/index.css` - Custom utilities (glassmorphism, shadows, animations) - `src/components/Widget.tsx` - Reference implementation (bento card pattern) --- ## 🛠️ Design Workflow ### 1. Discovery (Understand the Problem) ```markdown **User Story:** As a [type of user] I want to [goal] So that [benefit] **Current Pain Points:** - [Problem 1] - [Problem 2] **Success Metrics:** - [How we'll measure improvement] ``` ### 2. Ideation (Explore Solutions) - Sketch 3-5 different approaches (low fidelity) - Evaluate against constraints (brand, performance, accessibility) - Pick the best solution (explain why) ### 3. Design (Create High-Fidelity Mockup) - Use Neuman Brain design system - Show all states (default, hover, active, disabled, loading, error) - Annotate with spacing, colors, interactions ### 4. Review (Critique Against Standards) - Run through Design Critique Framework - Check accessibility (contrast, keyboard, screen reader) - Verify performance (GPU-accelerated animations) ### 5. Implement (Code) - Use existing components where possible - Follow Tailwind conventions - Add TypeScript types - Test keyboard navigation ### 6. Validate (User Testing) - Watch real users interact - Identify friction points - Iterate based on feedback --- ## ✅ Quick Decision Matrix **When designing, use this matrix to make quick decisions:** | Situation | Design Decision | Reasoning | |-----------|----------------|-----------| | Primary CTA on page | 48px height, magenta gradient, prominent placement | Fitts's Law (size/distance), Visual hierarchy | | Destructive action (delete) | Red accent, confirm dialog, separated from other actions | Error prevention, Visual distinction | | Form with 10+ fields | Multi-step wizard (3-5 fields per step) | Reduce cognitive load, Progressive disclosure | | Loading data (< 1s) | Skeleton screen | Better perceived performance than spinner | | Loading data (> 3s) | Progress bar with percentage | Manage expectations, show progress | | Icon-only button | 44px minimum touch target, aria-label | Accessibility (Fitts's Law, Screen readers) | | Hover effect | Subtle lift (4px) + glow, 200ms transition | Affordance, Smooth feedback | | Modal open/close | Fade overlay (200ms) → Slide content (300ms) | Follow-through, Smooth transitions | | Error message | Red icon + bold title + actionable steps + retry button | Clear communication, Error recovery | | Empty state | Illustration + title + description + primary CTA | Guide user, Reduce confusion | --- ## 🚨 Red Flags (When to Push Back) **As a UX designer, speak up when you see:** ❌ **Dark Patterns** - Tricks to manipulate users - Hidden unsubscribe links - Confusing double negatives ("Don't not send me emails") - Fake urgency ("Only 1 left!") ❌ **Accessibility Violations** - No keyboard navigation - Low color contrast (< 4.5:1) - Missing ARIA labels on icon buttons ❌ **Performance Issues** - Layout-triggering animations - Blocking the main thread - Unnecessary re-renders ❌ **Poor Usability** - More than 7 choices without grouping (Hick's Law) - Critical actions require >3 clicks - No undo for destructive actions ❌ **Inconsistency** - Different button styles for same actions - Inconsistent terminology - Different spacing/layout patterns **How to Push Back (Professionally):** ``` "I recommend against [X] because: 1. [Specific issue with user impact] 2. [Alternative solution] 3. [Expected improvement] Would you like to explore the alternative?" ``` --- ## 🎓 Continuous Learning **Stay sharp as a designer:** 1. **Critique Existing Apps** - Use frameworks above to analyze popular apps 2. **Follow Design Leaders** - Nielsen Norman Group, Smashing Magazine, A List Apart 3. **Study Psychology** - User behavior, cognitive biases, perception 4. **Learn from Users** - Watch real people use the app, collect feedback 5. **Experiment** - Try new patterns, A/B test, measure impact --- **Remember:** Great design is invisible. Users should accomplish their goals effortlessly, without thinking about the interface. **Your job:** Remove friction, add delight, empower users. --- **Last Updated:** November 14, 2025 **Framework:** User-Centered Design + Jobs-to-be-Done + Nielsen's Heuristics **Philosophy:** "Make it work, make it right, make it fast, make it beautiful."

# Neuman Brain Claude Skills

Custom skills optimized for Neuman Brain development following CLAUDE.md workflow and design principles.

**Last Updated:** November 19, 2025
**Total Active Skills:** 10 (4 Neuman Brain-specific + 4 customized + 2 unchanged document skills)

---

## 🎨 Design & UX Skills

### neuman-brain-design-system
**Purpose:** Complete design system reference - brand colors, typography, spacing, component patterns

**When to Use:**
- Implementing UI components
- Choosing colors/spacing values
- Creating animations
- Ensuring brand consistency
- Checking accessibility (WCAG AA)

**Key Content:**
- Brand colors: Magenta (#E91E8C), Cyan (#00C9FF), Purple gradients
- Typography: Inter font stack, responsive type scale
- Spacing: 4px base unit system
- Components: Widget, Modal, Button patterns
- Animations: GPU-accelerated (transform/opacity only)
- Glassmorphism effects and shadows

**Invoke:** Automatically when UI/styling context appears

---

### neuman-brain-ux-designer
**Purpose:** Professional UI/UX design expertise and design thinking

**When to Use:**
- Designing new features
- Improving existing UX
- Solving user problems
- Creating interaction patterns
- Optimizing perceived performance
- Conducting design reviews

**Key Content:**
- Design thinking framework (Jobs-to-be-Done)
- User psychology (Hick's Law, Fitts's Law, Jakob's Law)
- Visual hierarchy and gestalt principles
- Interaction design patterns
- Accessibility beyond WCAG
- Performance-driven design
- Design critique framework

**Invoke:** Request explicitly: "Use neuman-brain-ux-designer skill to critique this design"

---

## 🛠️ Development Skills

### neuman-brain-code-reviewer
**Purpose:** Comprehensive code review enforcing CLAUDE.md workflow and production quality

**When to Use:**
- Before committing code
- Completing features
- Reviewing pull requests
- Checking workflow compliance

**Key Checks:**
- CLAUDE.md workflow compliance (task files, reviews, tests)
- Security (no XSS, type safety, input sanitization)
- Performance (bundle size, GPU animations)
- Accessibility (WCAG AA, keyboard nav, ARIA)
- Privacy-first (IndexedDB, local-first)
- Design system compliance
- Surgical simplicity (minimal code impact)

**Invoke:** Automatically before commits, or request: "Review this code for Neuman Brain compliance"

---

### neuman-brain-feature-developer
**Purpose:** Guide feature development with architecture patterns and best practices

**When to Use:**
- Adding new features
- Refactoring existing code
- Planning major changes
- Understanding architecture

**Key Content:**
- Zustand store patterns
- IndexedDB usage and quota management
- Widget system and lazy loading
- Feature integration checklist
- Common patterns (stores, widgets, IndexedDB)
- Performance guidelines

**Invoke:** Request: "Help me develop [feature] following Neuman Brain architecture"

---

## 📝 Documentation & Analysis Skills

### codebase-documenter
**Purpose:** Create comprehensive, beginner-friendly documentation

**Customized for Neuman Brain:**
- Neuman Brain docs structure (`docs/technical/`, `docs/product/`, etc.)
- Privacy-first documentation (IndexedDB, offline capabilities)
- Performance documentation (bundle size, lazy loading)
- TypeScript strict mode examples
- Zustand store patterns
- Design system references

**When to Use:**
- Writing README files
- Creating architecture docs
- Adding code comments
- Documenting APIs
- Making codebase accessible

**Invoke:** Request: "Document this feature for Neuman Brain"

---

### tech-debt-analyzer
**Purpose:** Identify technical debt and CLAUDE.md workflow violations

**Customized for Neuman Brain:**
- CLAUDE.md compliance checks (task files, reviews, dates)
- Workflow debt (missing task files, unarchived tasks)
- Code quality debt (root cause not fixed, surgical simplicity violated)
- Neuman Brain-specific checks (privacy-first, performance, design system)

**When to Use:**
- Analyzing codebase health
- Creating technical debt register
- Assessing code maintainability
- Checking CLAUDE.md compliance

**Invoke:** Request: "Analyze technical debt in [area]"

---

### test-specialist
**Purpose:** Write comprehensive tests using Vitest + React Testing Library

**Customized for Neuman Brain:**
- Vitest patterns (not Jest)
- React Testing Library best practices
- User-centric queries (avoid implementation details)
- Test coverage requirements (Rule #13)
- Zustand store testing
- IndexedDB service testing
- Accessibility testing (keyboard nav, ARIA)

**When to Use:**
- Writing unit tests
- Adding regression tests for bugs
- Improving test coverage
- Debugging test failures

**Invoke:** Request: "Write tests for this component using Vitest"

---

### frontend-enhancer
**Purpose:** Generic Next.js frontend enhancement patterns

**⚠️ OVERRIDE WARNING:**
Neuman Brain has its own design system. This skill provides generic templates - always adapt to Neuman Brain requirements using `neuman-brain-design-system` and `neuman-brain-ux-designer`.

**When to Use:**
- Generic UI patterns (adapt to Neuman Brain)
- Next.js best practices
- Responsive layouts

**Invoke:** Use with caution, prefer Neuman Brain-specific skills

---

## 📄 Document Skills

### document-skills (4 sub-skills)
**Purpose:** Official Anthropic document manipulation skills

**Sub-skills:**
- `docx` - Word document creation and editing
- `pdf` - PDF manipulation and forms
- `pptx` - PowerPoint presentation creation
- `xlsx` - Excel spreadsheet operations

**When to Use:**
- Creating/editing documents
- Generating reports
- Exporting data

**Invoke:** Automatically when document context appears

---

## 🔄 How Skills Work Together

**Example: Adding New Widget**

1. **neuman-brain-feature-developer** → Architecture guidance (Zustand, Widget system)
2. **neuman-brain-ux-designer** → Design interaction patterns
3. **neuman-brain-design-system** → Apply colors, spacing, components
4. **test-specialist** → Write Vitest tests
5. **neuman-brain-code-reviewer** → Review before commit
6. **codebase-documenter** → Update docs

---

## 📖 Skill Invocation

**Automatic:**
Skills activate automatically when relevant context appears (e.g., design system loads when implementing UI)

**Explicit:**
Request skills directly:
- "Use neuman-brain-ux-designer to critique this layout"
- "Use neuman-brain-code-reviewer to review this code"
- "Use neuman-brain-feature-developer to help me build [feature]"

---

## 🗂️ Removed Skills (November 19, 2025)

Removed 19 generic skills not relevant to Neuman Brain development:
- brand-analyzer, business-analytics-reporter, business-document-generator
- cicd-pipeline-generator, csv-data-visualizer, data-analyst
- docker-containerization, finance-manager, nutritional-specialist
- personal-assistant, pitch-deck, research-paper-writer
- resume-manager, script-writer, seo-optimizer
- social-media-generator, startup-validator, storyboard-manager
- travel-planner

**Reason:** Focus on skills directly supporting Neuman Brain development workflow.

---

## 📚 See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete workflow and development principles
- `docs/technical/development_principles.md` - Privacy/security/UX requirements
- `docs/product/feature_roadmap.md` - Product vision and roadmap
- `.claude/automation-guide.md` - Workflow automation tools

---

**Questions?**
- Skills not working? Check skill names match exactly (lowercase, hyphens)
- Need new skill? Follow `.claude/templates/skill-template.md` pattern
- Skill conflicts? Review skill descriptions for proper use cases

content.content:
1→# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide
     2→
     3→**Version:** 1.0
     4→**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
     5→**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Table of Contents
    10→
    11→1. [Overview](#overview)
    12→2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
    13→3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
    14→4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
    15→5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
    16→6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
    17→7. [Customization](#customization)
    18→8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)
    19→
    20→---
    21→
    22→## Overview
    23→
    24→This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md rules #1-11 by:
    25→
    26→✅ **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
    27→✅ **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
    28→✅ **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
    29→✅ **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists
    30→
    31→**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.
    32→
    33→---
    34→
    35→## Quick Start
    36→
    37→### For Developers
    38→
    39→1. **Before starting any task:**
    40→   ```bash
    41→   # Check workflow checklist
    42→   cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
    43→   ```
    44→
    45→2. **Create task file from template:**
    46→   ```bash
    47→   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
    48→   ```
    49→
    50→3. **Git hook automatically enforces:**
    51→   - Task file must be staged when committing code changes
    52→   - Will block commit if missing
    53→
    54→### For Claude Code
    55→
    56→1. **Use `/start-task` command** (if available)
    57→2. **Reference workflow checklist** before starting
    58→3. **Use task template** for all new tasks
    59→4. **Git hook will catch** if task file missing
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→## Automation Tools
    64→
    65→### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook
    66→
    67→**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
    68→**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
    69→**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7
    70→
    71→**How it works:**
    72→1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
    73→2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
    74→3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
    75→4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)
    76→
    77→**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
    78→```
    79→━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    80→  ❌ COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
    81→━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    82→
    83→Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.
    84→
    85→Changed code files:
    86→  - src/components/Modal.tsx
    87→  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
    88→
    89→Required Action:
    90→  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
    91→  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
    92→  3. Retry commit: git commit
    93→```
    94→
    95→**Emergency Bypass:**
    96→```bash
    97→git commit --no-verify
    98→```
    99→⚠️ **Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies
   100→
   101→---
   102→
   103→### 2. Workflow Checklist
   104→
   105→**Location:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   106→**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
   107→**Enforces:** All rules #1-11
   108→
   109→**When to use:**
   110→- Before starting ANY task
   111→- During implementation (reference as needed)
   112→- Before marking work complete
   113→- Before creating git commits
   114→
   115→**Sections:**
   116→- ✅ Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
   117→- ✅ During Implementation
   118→- ✅ When Complete
   119→- ✅ Git Commit Checklist
   120→- ✅ Rules #1-11 Quick Reference
   121→
   122→**View checklist:**
   123→```bash
   124→cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   125→```
   126→
   127→---
   128→
   129→### 3. Task Template
   130→
   131→**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   132→**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
   133→**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7
   134→
   135→**Sections included:**
   136→- Overview and problem statement
   137→- Files to read
   138→- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
   139→- Todo checklist with time estimates
   140→- Design considerations
   141→- Implementation notes
   142→- Testing checklist
   143→- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)
   144→
   145→**Create new task:**
   146→```bash
   147→cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   148→```
   149→
   150→**Good naming examples:**
   151→- ✅ `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
   152→- ✅ `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
   153→- ✅ `tasks/notes-ux-improvements.md`
   154→
   155→**Bad naming examples:**
   156→- ❌ `tasks/todo.md`
   157→- ❌ `tasks/task1.md`
   158→- ❌ `tasks/new.md`
   159→
   160→---
   161→
   162→### 4. Custom Commands
   163→
   164→**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
   165→**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow reminders
   166→
   167→#### `/start-task` Command
   168→
   169→**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
   170→**Purpose:** Comprehensive reminder before starting any task
   171→
   172→**What it covers:**
   173→1. Sync with main branch (Rule #11)
   174→2. Read CLAUDE.md (Rules #1-11)
   175→3. Create task file from template
   176→4. Fill out task plan
   177→5. Get user approval (Rule #3)
   178→6. Implementation workflow
   179→7. Review section and documentation updates
   180→
   181→**Use when:**
   182→- Starting a new task
   183→- Unsure of the workflow
   184→- Need a quick reference
   185→
   186→---
   187→
   188→## Setup Instructions
   189→
   190→### Initial Setup (One-Time)
   191→
   192→The automation tools are already set up in this repository. No additional installation required.
   193→
   194→**Verify setup:**
   195→```bash
   196→# Check git hook exists and is executable
   197→ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   198→
   199→# Check automation files exist
   200→ls -la .claude/
   201→
   202→# Expected output:
   203→# .claude/
   204→# ├── automation-guide.md         (this file)
   205→# ├── workflow-checklist.md       (step-by-step checklist)
   206→# ├── templates/
   207→# │   └── task-template.md        (task file template)
   208→# └── commands/
   209→#     └── start-task.md           (custom command)
   210→```
   211→
   212→### For New Developers
   213→
   214→1. **Clone repository**
   215→2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (especially rules #1-11)
   216→3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
   217→4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   218→5. **Test git hook:**
   219→   ```bash
   220→   # Make a small change to src/ file
   221→   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx
   222→
   223→   # Try to commit without staging task file
   224→   git add src/main.tsx
   225→   git commit -m "test"
   226→   # Should be BLOCKED
   227→
   228→   # Stage task file and retry
   229→   git add tasks/some-task.md
   230→   git commit -m "test"
   231→   # Should succeed
   232→
   233→   # Revert test
   234→   git reset HEAD~1
   235→   git checkout src/main.tsx
   236→   ```
   237→
   238→---
   239→
   240→## How It Works
   241→
   242→### Git Hook Workflow
   243→
   244→```
   245→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   246→│  Developer runs: git commit                                  │
   247→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   248→                     │
   249→                     ▼
   250→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   251→│  Pre-commit hook executes (.git/hooks/pre-commit)           │
   252→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   253→                     │
   254→                     ▼
   255→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   256→│  Check: Are any src/ files staged?                          │
   257→│  (src/, components/, widgets/, stores/, services/)          │
   258→└────────────┬────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┘
   259→             │ YES                    │ NO
   260→             ▼                        ▼
   261→┌──────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
   262→│ Check: Is tasks/*.md     │  │ Allow commit                 │
   263→│ file staged?             │  │ (doc-only, config, etc.)     │
   264→└──────┬───────────┬───────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
   265→       │ YES       │ NO
   266→       ▼           ▼
   267→┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   268→│ ✅ Allow   │  │ ❌ BLOCK commit                             │
   269→│ commit     │  │ Display error message with instructions     │
   270→└────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   271→```
   272→
   273→### Task File Lifecycle
   274→
   275→```
   276→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   277→│  1. Start Task                                               │
   278→│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
   279→│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
   280→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   281→                     │
   282→                     ▼
   283→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   284→│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
   285→│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
   286→│     - Create todo checklist with time estimates             │
   287→│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
   288→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   289→                     │
   290→                     ▼
   291→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   292→│  3. Implementation                                           │
   293→│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
   294→│     - Add implementation notes                               │
   295→│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
   296→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   297→                     │
   298→                     ▼
   299→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   300→│  4. Completion                                               │
   301→│     - Fill out review section                                │
   302→│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
   303→│     - Add testing status, time comparison                    │
   304→│     - Update docs/ if architecture changed                   │
   305→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   306→                     │
   307→                     ▼
   308→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   309→│  5. Commit                                                   │
   310→│     - Stage code files: git add src/                        │
   311→│     - Stage task file: git add tasks/[feature].md          │
   312→│     - Pre-commit hook verifies both staged                   │
   313→│     - Commit succeeds ✅                                     │
   314→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   315→```
   316→
   317→---
   318→
   319→## Troubleshooting
   320→
   321→### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running
   322→
   323→**Symptoms:**
   324→- Can commit code without task file
   325→- No error message appears
   326→
   327→**Causes & Fixes:**
   328→
   329→**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
   330→```bash
   331→# Fix: Make it executable
   332→chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
   333→```
   334→
   335→**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
   336→```bash
   337→# Check if file exists
   338→ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   339→
   340→# If missing, recreate from template (if you have one)
   341→# Or re-run setup script
   342→```
   343→
   344→**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
   345→```bash
   346→# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
   347→git commit  # ✅ Correct
   348→git commit --no-verify  # ❌ Bypasses hook
   349→```
   350→
   351→**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
   352→```bash
   353→# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe)
   354→# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
   355→```
   356→
   357→---
   358→
   359→### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits
   360→
   361→**Symptoms:**
   362→- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
   363→- False positive error
   364→
   365→**Causes & Fixes:**
   366→
   367→**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
   368→```bash
   369→# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
   370→git add tasks/my-feature.md  # ✅ Correct
   371→git add my-feature.md         # ❌ Wrong location
   372→git add docs/task.md          # ❌ Wrong location
   373→```
   374→
   375→**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
   376→```bash
   377→# Check what's staged
   378→git diff --cached --name-only
   379→
   380→# Ensure task file is in the list
   381→# If not, stage it:
   382→git add tasks/my-feature.md
   383→```
   384→
   385→**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
   386→```bash
   387→# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
   388→git add tasks/my-feature.md   # ✅ Correct
   389→git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # ❌ Wrong extension
   390→```
   391→
   392→---
   393→
   394→### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template
   395→
   396→**Symptoms:**
   397→- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
   398→- File not found error
   399→
   400→**Fixes:**
   401→```bash
   402→# Check if template exists
   403→ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md
   404→
   405→# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
   406→ls -la .claude/
   407→
   408→# Ensure you're in repository root
   409→pwd  # Should end with system.knowledgebase
   410→```
   411→
   412→---
   413→
   414→### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File
   415→
   416→**Symptoms:**
   417→- Code commits go through without task file
   418→- Hook seems to not detect code changes
   419→
   420→**Causes & Fixes:**
   421→
   422→**Cause 1:** Code not in src/ directory
   423→```bash
   424→# Hook only checks src/ directory
   425→# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it
   426→
   427→# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
   428→# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
   429→# Find line: CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/')
   430→# Add more patterns: grep -E '^(src/|lib/|components/)'
   431→```
   432→
   433→**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
   434→```bash
   435→# Hook ignores .md files in src/
   436→# (to allow README updates without task file)
   437→
   438→# This is intentional behavior
   439→```
   440→
   441→---
   442→
   443→### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying
   444→
   445→**Symptoms:**
   446→- Commit blocked but no error message shown
   447→- Silent failure
   448→
   449→**Fixes:**
   450→```bash
   451→# Test hook manually
   452→bash .git/hooks/pre-commit
   453→
   454→# Check for syntax errors
   455→bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit
   456→
   457→# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
   458→# Some terminals don't support color codes
   459→```
   460→
   461→---
   462→
   463→## Customization
   464→
   465→### Adding More File Patterns to Hook
   466→
   467→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)
   468→
   469→```bash
   470→# Current pattern (only src/)
   471→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')
   472→
   473→# Add more directories (lib/, config/, etc.)
   474→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
   475→```
   476→
   477→### Excluding Specific Files from Hook
   478→
   479→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)
   480→
   481→```bash
   482→# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
   483→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$)')
   484→```
   485→
   486→### Customizing Task Template
   487→
   488→**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   489→
   490→Add/remove sections as needed:
   491→- Additional checklists
   492→- Project-specific requirements
   493→- Custom metadata fields
   494→
   495→**Example additions:**
   496→```markdown
   497→## Security Checklist
   498→- [ ] Input validation added
   499→- [ ] XSS prevention verified
   500→- [ ] SQL injection prevented
   501→
   502→## Performance Checklist
   503→- [ ] Bundle size impact < 10KB
   504→- [ ] No memory leaks
   505→- [ ] Animations 60fps
   506→```
   507→
   508→### Adding New Custom Commands
   509→
   510→**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`
   511→
   512→Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`
   513→
   514→```markdown
   515→# Review Task Checklist
   516→
   517→Before marking task complete:
   518→
   519→- [ ] Review section filled out
   520→- [ ] All files documented
   521→- [ ] Testing complete
   522→- [ ] Documentation updated
   523→- [ ] Bundle size checked
   524→```
   525→
   526→### Adjusting Hook Error Messages
   527→
   528→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Lines 30-70)
   529→
   530→Customize:
   531→- Error message text
   532→- Color scheme (ANSI codes)
   533→- Instructions provided
   534→- Examples shown
   535→
   536→---
   537→
   538→## Best Practices
   539→
   540→### 1. Always Start with the Checklist
   541→
   542→Before starting any task:
   543→```bash
   544→# Read the workflow checklist
   545→cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   546→
   547→# Or use custom command
   548→/start-task  # If available in Claude Code
   549→```
   550→
   551→### 2. Create Task File FIRST
   552→
   553→```bash
   554→# ✅ CORRECT ORDER
   555→cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
   556→# Fill out the task file
   557→# THEN start coding
   558→
   559→# ❌ WRONG ORDER
   560→# Start coding first
   561→# Create task file later (will forget details!)
   562→```
   563→
   564→### 3. Update Task File as You Work
   565→
   566→```markdown
   567→## Todo Checklist
   568→
   569→### Phase 1
   570→- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE (took 30 min)
   571→- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
   572→- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for user input)
   573→```
   574→
   575→Keep the task file up to date, don't batch updates at the end.
   576→
   577→### 4. Fill Out Review Section Immediately
   578→
   579→When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
   580→- Summary of changes
   581→- Files created/modified
   582→- Testing status
   583→- Issues encountered
   584→
   585→Don't wait days/weeks - you'll forget important details!
   586→
   587→### 5. Stage Task File with Code
   588→
   589→```bash
   590→# ✅ CORRECT - Stage both together
   591→git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   592→git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
   593→git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"
   594→
   595→# ❌ WRONG - Separate commits
   596→git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   597→git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
   598→# Oops, forgot task file! (Hook will block this)
   599→```
   600→
   601→### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names
   602→
   603→```bash
   604→# ✅ GOOD - Descriptive, specific
   605→tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
   606→tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
   607→tasks/refactor-zustand-stores.md
   608→
   609→# ❌ BAD - Vague, generic
   610→tasks/todo.md
   611→tasks/task1.md
   612→tasks/stuff.md
   613→tasks/changes.md
   614→```
   615→
   616→### 7. One Task File Per Feature
   617→
   618→Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.
   619→
   620→```bash
   621→# ✅ CORRECT - Separate files
   622→tasks/add-search-feature.md
   623→tasks/add-export-feature.md
   624→
   625→# ❌ WRONG - One giant file
   626→tasks/all-features.md
   627→```
   628→
   629→### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES
   630→
   631→```bash
   632→# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
   633→git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
   634→git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
   635→git commit --no-verify  # Doc-only changes (maybe)
   636→
   637→# NOT for:
   638→git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
   639→git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later" (you won't!)
   640→```
   641→
   642→### 9. Keep Documentation Updated
   643→
   644→If architecture changes, update docs:
   645→```bash
   646→git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
   647→git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
   648→git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md  # New architecture doc
   649→git commit -m "feat: Add undo system
   650→
   651→Closes #42"
   652→```
   653→
   654→### 10. Reference CLAUDE.md Rules
   655→
   656→When in doubt, check CLAUDE.md:
   657→```bash
   658→# Quick reference
   659→cat CLAUDE.md | grep "^[0-9])"
   660→
   661→# Full read
   662→cat CLAUDE.md
   663→```
   664→
   665→---
   666→
   667→## Integration with Claude Code
   668→
   669→### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code
   670→
   671→1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
   672→2. **Use task template** for all planning
   673→3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
   674→4. **Fill review section** when done
   675→5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged
   676→
   677→### Behavioral Changes for Claude Code
   678→
   679→**Before starting ANY task:**
   680→- ✅ Read `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   681→- ✅ Create task file from template
   682→- ✅ Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)
   683→
   684→**During implementation:**
   685→- ✅ Update todos as you complete them
   686→- ✅ Document decisions in task file
   687→- ✅ Track time estimates vs actual
   688→
   689→**When complete:**
   690→- ✅ Fill out review section
   691→- ✅ Update docs/ if architecture changed
   692→- ✅ Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)
   693→
   694→**On every commit:**
   695→- ✅ Stage code files AND task file together
   696→- ✅ Git hook verifies compliance
   697→- ✅ Commit proceeds
   698→
   699→---
   700→
   701→## Appendix
   702→
   703→### File Structure Reference
   704→
   705→```
   706→system.knowledgebase/
   707→├── .git/
   708→│   └── hooks/
   709→│       └── pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
   710→├── .claude/
   711→│   ├── automation-guide.md         # This file
   712→│   ├── workflow-checklist.md       # Step-by-step checklist
   713→│   ├── templates/
   714→│   │   └── task-template.md        # Task file template
   715→│   └── commands/
   716→│       └── start-task.md           # Custom command
   717→├── tasks/
   718→│   └── [feature-name].md           # Task files (one per feature)
   719→├── CLAUDE.md                        # Main workflow guide (rules #1-11)
   720→└── README.md                        # User-facing documentation
   721→```
   722→
   723→### Git Hook Exit Codes
   724→
   725→- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
   726→- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)
   727→
   728→### ANSI Color Codes Used
   729→
   730→```bash
   731→RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
   732→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
   733→GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
   734→BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
   735→NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
   736→```
   737→
   738→---
   739→
   740→## Support & Questions
   741→
   742→**For questions about:**
   743→- **CLAUDE.md workflow:** See `CLAUDE.md` in root
   744→- **Workflow steps:** See `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   745→- **Task templates:** See `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   746→- **Git hook issues:** See troubleshooting section above
   747→- **Feature requests:** Create GitHub issue
   748→
   749→---
   750→
   751→**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
   752→**Maintained By:** Engineering Team
   753→**Version:** 1.0
   754→
   755→**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. They catch mistakes BEFORE they become problems. Follow the workflow, and your development process will be smoother and more consistent.
   756→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide

**Version:** 1.0
**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview](#overview)
2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
7. [Customization](#customization)
8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)

---

## Overview

This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md rules #1-11 by:

✅ **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
✅ **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
✅ **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
✅ **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists

**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.

---

## Quick Start

### For Developers

1. **Before starting any task:**
   ```bash
   # Check workflow checklist
   cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   ```

2. **Create task file from template:**
   ```bash
   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   ```

3. **Git hook automatically enforces:**
   - Task file must be staged when committing code changes
   - Will block commit if missing

### For Claude Code

1. **Use `/start-task` command** (if available)
2. **Reference workflow checklist** before starting
3. **Use task template** for all new tasks
4. **Git hook will catch** if task file missing

---

## Automation Tools

### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook

**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7

**How it works:**
1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)

**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  ❌ COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.

Changed code files:
  - src/components/Modal.tsx
  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts

Required Action:
  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
  3. Retry commit: git commit
```

**Emergency Bypass:**
```bash
git commit --no-verify
```
⚠️ **Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies

---

### 2. Workflow Checklist

**Location:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
**Enforces:** All rules #1-11

**When to use:**
- Before starting ANY task
- During implementation (reference as needed)
- Before marking work complete
- Before creating git commits

**Sections:**
- ✅ Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
- ✅ During Implementation
- ✅ When Complete
- ✅ Git Commit Checklist
- ✅ Rules #1-11 Quick Reference

**View checklist:**
```bash
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
```

---

### 3. Task Template

**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7

**Sections included:**
- Overview and problem statement
- Files to read
- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
- Todo checklist with time estimates
- Design considerations
- Implementation notes
- Testing checklist
- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)

**Create new task:**
```bash
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Good naming examples:**
- ✅ `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
- ✅ `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
- ✅ `tasks/notes-ux-improvements.md`

**Bad naming examples:**
- ❌ `tasks/todo.md`
- ❌ `tasks/task1.md`
- ❌ `tasks/new.md`

---

### 4. Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow reminders

#### `/start-task` Command

**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
**Purpose:** Comprehensive reminder before starting any task

**What it covers:**
1. Sync with main branch (Rule #11)
2. Read CLAUDE.md (Rules #1-11)
3. Create task file from template
4. Fill out task plan
5. Get user approval (Rule #3)
6. Implementation workflow
7. Review section and documentation updates

**Use when:**
- Starting a new task
- Unsure of the workflow
- Need a quick reference

---

## Setup Instructions

### Initial Setup (One-Time)

The automation tools are already set up in this repository. No additional installation required.

**Verify setup:**
```bash
# Check git hook exists and is executable
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check automation files exist
ls -la .claude/

# Expected output:
# .claude/
# ├── automation-guide.md         (this file)
# ├── workflow-checklist.md       (step-by-step checklist)
# ├── templates/
# │   └── task-template.md        (task file template)
# └── commands/
#     └── start-task.md           (custom command)
```

### For New Developers

1. **Clone repository**
2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (especially rules #1-11)
3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
5. **Test git hook:**
   ```bash
   # Make a small change to src/ file
   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx

   # Try to commit without staging task file
   git add src/main.tsx
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should be BLOCKED

   # Stage task file and retry
   git add tasks/some-task.md
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should succeed

   # Revert test
   git reset HEAD~1
   git checkout src/main.tsx
   ```

---

## How It Works

### Git Hook Workflow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Developer runs: git commit                                  │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Pre-commit hook executes (.git/hooks/pre-commit)           │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Check: Are any src/ files staged?                          │
│  (src/, components/, widgets/, stores/, services/)          │
└────────────┬────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┘
             │ YES                    │ NO
             ▼                        ▼
┌──────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ Check: Is tasks/*.md     │  │ Allow commit                 │
│ file staged?             │  │ (doc-only, config, etc.)     │
└──────┬───────────┬───────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
       │ YES       │ NO
       ▼           ▼
┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ✅ Allow   │  │ ❌ BLOCK commit                             │
│ commit     │  │ Display error message with instructions     │
└────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Task File Lifecycle

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. Start Task                                               │
│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
│     - Create todo checklist with time estimates             │
│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  3. Implementation                                           │
│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
│     - Add implementation notes                               │
│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  4. Completion                                               │
│     - Fill out review section                                │
│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
│     - Add testing status, time comparison                    │
│     - Update docs/ if architecture changed                   │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  5. Commit                                                   │
│     - Stage code files: git add src/                        │
│     - Stage task file: git add tasks/[feature].md          │
│     - Pre-commit hook verifies both staged                   │
│     - Commit succeeds ✅                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Troubleshooting

### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running

**Symptoms:**
- Can commit code without task file
- No error message appears

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
```bash
# Fix: Make it executable
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
```

**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
```bash
# Check if file exists
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# If missing, recreate from template (if you have one)
# Or re-run setup script
```

**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
```bash
# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
git commit  # ✅ Correct
git commit --no-verify  # ❌ Bypasses hook
```

**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
```bash
# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe)
# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
```

---

### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits

**Symptoms:**
- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
- False positive error

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
```bash
# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
git add tasks/my-feature.md  # ✅ Correct
git add my-feature.md         # ❌ Wrong location
git add docs/task.md          # ❌ Wrong location
```

**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
```bash
# Check what's staged
git diff --cached --name-only

# Ensure task file is in the list
# If not, stage it:
git add tasks/my-feature.md
```

**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
```bash
# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
git add tasks/my-feature.md   # ✅ Correct
git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # ❌ Wrong extension
```

---

### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template

**Symptoms:**
- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
- File not found error

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Check if template exists
ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md

# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
ls -la .claude/

# Ensure you're in repository root
pwd  # Should end with system.knowledgebase
```

---

### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File

**Symptoms:**
- Code commits go through without task file
- Hook seems to not detect code changes

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Code not in src/ directory
```bash
# Hook only checks src/ directory
# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it

# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
# Find line: CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/')
# Add more patterns: grep -E '^(src/|lib/|components/)'
```

**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
```bash
# Hook ignores .md files in src/
# (to allow README updates without task file)

# This is intentional behavior
```

---

### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying

**Symptoms:**
- Commit blocked but no error message shown
- Silent failure

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Test hook manually
bash .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check for syntax errors
bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
# Some terminals don't support color codes
```

---

## Customization

### Adding More File Patterns to Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)

```bash
# Current pattern (only src/)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')

# Add more directories (lib/, config/, etc.)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
```

### Excluding Specific Files from Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)

```bash
# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$)')
```

### Customizing Task Template

**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`

Add/remove sections as needed:
- Additional checklists
- Project-specific requirements
- Custom metadata fields

**Example additions:**
```markdown
## Security Checklist
- [ ] Input validation added
- [ ] XSS prevention verified
- [ ] SQL injection prevented

## Performance Checklist
- [ ] Bundle size impact < 10KB
- [ ] No memory leaks
- [ ] Animations 60fps
```

### Adding New Custom Commands

**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`

Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`

```markdown
# Review Task Checklist

Before marking task complete:

- [ ] Review section filled out
- [ ] All files documented
- [ ] Testing complete
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Bundle size checked
```

### Adjusting Hook Error Messages

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Lines 30-70)

Customize:
- Error message text
- Color scheme (ANSI codes)
- Instructions provided
- Examples shown

---

## Best Practices

### 1. Always Start with the Checklist

Before starting any task:
```bash
# Read the workflow checklist
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md

# Or use custom command
/start-task  # If available in Claude Code
```

### 2. Create Task File FIRST

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT ORDER
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
# Fill out the task file
# THEN start coding

# ❌ WRONG ORDER
# Start coding first
# Create task file later (will forget details!)
```

### 3. Update Task File as You Work

```markdown
## Todo Checklist

### Phase 1
- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE (took 30 min)
- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for user input)
```

Keep the task file up to date, don't batch updates at the end.

### 4. Fill Out Review Section Immediately

When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
- Summary of changes
- Files created/modified
- Testing status
- Issues encountered

Don't wait days/weeks - you'll forget important details!

### 5. Stage Task File with Code

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT - Stage both together
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"

# ❌ WRONG - Separate commits
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
# Oops, forgot task file! (Hook will block this)
```

### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names

```bash
# ✅ GOOD - Descriptive, specific
tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
tasks/refactor-zustand-stores.md

# ❌ BAD - Vague, generic
tasks/todo.md
tasks/task1.md
tasks/stuff.md
tasks/changes.md
```

### 7. One Task File Per Feature

Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT - Separate files
tasks/add-search-feature.md
tasks/add-export-feature.md

# ❌ WRONG - One giant file
tasks/all-features.md
```

### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES

```bash
# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
git commit --no-verify  # Doc-only changes (maybe)

# NOT for:
git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later" (you won't!)
```

### 9. Keep Documentation Updated

If architecture changes, update docs:
```bash
git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md  # New architecture doc
git commit -m "feat: Add undo system

Closes #42"
```

### 10. Reference CLAUDE.md Rules

When in doubt, check CLAUDE.md:
```bash
# Quick reference
cat CLAUDE.md | grep "^[0-9])"

# Full read
cat CLAUDE.md
```

---

## Integration with Claude Code

### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code

1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
2. **Use task template** for all planning
3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
4. **Fill review section** when done
5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged

### Behavioral Changes for Claude Code

**Before starting ANY task:**
- ✅ Read `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
- ✅ Create task file from template
- ✅ Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)

**During implementation:**
- ✅ Update todos as you complete them
- ✅ Document decisions in task file
- ✅ Track time estimates vs actual

**When complete:**
- ✅ Fill out review section
- ✅ Update docs/ if architecture changed
- ✅ Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)

**On every commit:**
- ✅ Stage code files AND task file together
- ✅ Git hook verifies compliance
- ✅ Commit proceeds

---

## Appendix

### File Structure Reference

```
system.knowledgebase/
├── .git/
│   └── hooks/
│       └── pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
├── .claude/
│   ├── automation-guide.md         # This file
│   ├── workflow-checklist.md       # Step-by-step checklist
│   ├── templates/
│   │   └── task-template.md        # Task file template
│   └── commands/
│       └── start-task.md           # Custom command
├── tasks/
│   └── [feature-name].md           # Task files (one per feature)
├── CLAUDE.md                        # Main workflow guide (rules #1-11)
└── README.md                        # User-facing documentation
```

### Git Hook Exit Codes

- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)

### ANSI Color Codes Used

```bash
RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
```

---

## Support & Questions

**For questions about:**
- **CLAUDE.md workflow:** See `CLAUDE.md` in root
- **Workflow steps:** See `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
- **Task templates:** See `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
- **Git hook issues:** See troubleshooting section above
- **Feature requests:** Create GitHub issue

---

**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
**Maintained By:** Engineering Team
**Version:** 1.0

**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. They catch mistakes BEFORE they become problems. Follow the workflow, and your development process will be smoother and more consistent.
