1→# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide
     2→
     3→**Version:** 1.0
     4→**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
     5→**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Table of Contents
    10→
    11→1. [Overview](#overview)
    12→2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
    13→3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
    14→4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
    15→5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
    16→6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
    17→7. [Customization](#customization)
    18→8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)
    19→
    20→---
    21→
    22→## Overview
    23→
    24→This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md rules #1-11 by:
    25→
    26→✅ **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
    27→✅ **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
    28→✅ **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
    29→✅ **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists
    30→
    31→**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.
    32→
    33→---
    34→
    35→## Quick Start
    36→
    37→### For Developers
    38→
    39→1. **Before starting any task:**
    40→   ```bash
    41→   # Check workflow checklist
    42→   cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
    43→   ```
    44→
    45→2. **Create task file from template:**
    46→   ```bash
    47→   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
    48→   ```
    49→
    50→3. **Git hook automatically enforces:**
    51→   - Task file must be staged when committing code changes
    52→   - Will block commit if missing
    53→
    54→### For Claude Code
    55→
    56→1. **Use `/start-task` command** (if available)
    57→2. **Reference workflow checklist** before starting
    58→3. **Use task template** for all new tasks
    59→4. **Git hook will catch** if task file missing
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→## Automation Tools
    64→
    65→### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook
    66→
    67→**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
    68→**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
    69→**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7
    70→
    71→**How it works:**
    72→1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
    73→2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
    74→3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
    75→4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)
    76→
    77→**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
    78→```
    79→━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    80→  ❌ COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
    81→━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    82→
    83→Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.
    84→
    85→Changed code files:
    86→  - src/components/Modal.tsx
    87→  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
    88→
    89→Required Action:
    90→  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
    91→  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
    92→  3. Retry commit: git commit
    93→```
    94→
    95→**Emergency Bypass:**
    96→```bash
    97→git commit --no-verify
    98→```
    99→⚠️ **Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies
   100→
   101→---
   102→
   103→### 2. Workflow Checklist
   104→
   105→**Location:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   106→**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
   107→**Enforces:** All rules #1-11
   108→
   109→**When to use:**
   110→- Before starting ANY task
   111→- During implementation (reference as needed)
   112→- Before marking work complete
   113→- Before creating git commits
   114→
   115→**Sections:**
   116→- ✅ Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
   117→- ✅ During Implementation
   118→- ✅ When Complete
   119→- ✅ Git Commit Checklist
   120→- ✅ Rules #1-11 Quick Reference
   121→
   122→**View checklist:**
   123→```bash
   124→cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   125→```
   126→
   127→---
   128→
   129→### 3. Task Template
   130→
   131→**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   132→**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
   133→**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7
   134→
   135→**Sections included:**
   136→- Overview and problem statement
   137→- Files to read
   138→- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
   139→- Todo checklist with time estimates
   140→- Design considerations
   141→- Implementation notes
   142→- Testing checklist
   143→- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)
   144→
   145→**Create new task:**
   146→```bash
   147→cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   148→```
   149→
   150→**Good naming examples:**
   151→- ✅ `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
   152→- ✅ `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
   153→- ✅ `tasks/notes-ux-improvements.md`
   154→
   155→**Bad naming examples:**
   156→- ❌ `tasks/todo.md`
   157→- ❌ `tasks/task1.md`
   158→- ❌ `tasks/new.md`
   159→
   160→---
   161→
   162→### 4. Custom Commands
   163→
   164→**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
   165→**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow reminders
   166→
   167→#### `/start-task` Command
   168→
   169→**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
   170→**Purpose:** Comprehensive reminder before starting any task
   171→
   172→**What it covers:**
   173→1. Sync with main branch (Rule #11)
   174→2. Read CLAUDE.md (Rules #1-11)
   175→3. Create task file from template
   176→4. Fill out task plan
   177→5. Get user approval (Rule #3)
   178→6. Implementation workflow
   179→7. Review section and documentation updates
   180→
   181→**Use when:**
   182→- Starting a new task
   183→- Unsure of the workflow
   184→- Need a quick reference
   185→
   186→---
   187→
   188→## Setup Instructions
   189→
   190→### Initial Setup (One-Time)
   191→
   192→The automation tools are already set up in this repository. No additional installation required.
   193→
   194→**Verify setup:**
   195→```bash
   196→# Check git hook exists and is executable
   197→ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   198→
   199→# Check automation files exist
   200→ls -la .claude/
   201→
   202→# Expected output:
   203→# .claude/
   204→# ├── automation-guide.md         (this file)
   205→# ├── workflow-checklist.md       (step-by-step checklist)
   206→# ├── templates/
   207→# │   └── task-template.md        (task file template)
   208→# └── commands/
   209→#     └── start-task.md           (custom command)
   210→```
   211→
   212→### For New Developers
   213→
   214→1. **Clone repository**
   215→2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (especially rules #1-11)
   216→3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
   217→4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   218→5. **Test git hook:**
   219→   ```bash
   220→   # Make a small change to src/ file
   221→   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx
   222→
   223→   # Try to commit without staging task file
   224→   git add src/main.tsx
   225→   git commit -m "test"
   226→   # Should be BLOCKED
   227→
   228→   # Stage task file and retry
   229→   git add tasks/some-task.md
   230→   git commit -m "test"
   231→   # Should succeed
   232→
   233→   # Revert test
   234→   git reset HEAD~1
   235→   git checkout src/main.tsx
   236→   ```
   237→
   238→---
   239→
   240→## How It Works
   241→
   242→### Git Hook Workflow
   243→
   244→```
   245→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   246→│  Developer runs: git commit                                  │
   247→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   248→                     │
   249→                     ▼
   250→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   251→│  Pre-commit hook executes (.git/hooks/pre-commit)           │
   252→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   253→                     │
   254→                     ▼
   255→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   256→│  Check: Are any src/ files staged?                          │
   257→│  (src/, components/, widgets/, stores/, services/)          │
   258→└────────────┬────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┘
   259→             │ YES                    │ NO
   260→             ▼                        ▼
   261→┌──────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
   262→│ Check: Is tasks/*.md     │  │ Allow commit                 │
   263→│ file staged?             │  │ (doc-only, config, etc.)     │
   264→└──────┬───────────┬───────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
   265→       │ YES       │ NO
   266→       ▼           ▼
   267→┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   268→│ ✅ Allow   │  │ ❌ BLOCK commit                             │
   269→│ commit     │  │ Display error message with instructions     │
   270→└────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   271→```
   272→
   273→### Task File Lifecycle
   274→
   275→```
   276→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   277→│  1. Start Task                                               │
   278→│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
   279→│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
   280→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   281→                     │
   282→                     ▼
   283→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   284→│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
   285→│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
   286→│     - Create todo checklist with time estimates             │
   287→│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
   288→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   289→                     │
   290→                     ▼
   291→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   292→│  3. Implementation                                           │
   293→│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
   294→│     - Add implementation notes                               │
   295→│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
   296→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   297→                     │
   298→                     ▼
   299→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   300→│  4. Completion                                               │
   301→│     - Fill out review section                                │
   302→│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
   303→│     - Add testing status, time comparison                    │
   304→│     - Update docs/ if architecture changed                   │
   305→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   306→                     │
   307→                     ▼
   308→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   309→│  5. Commit                                                   │
   310→│     - Stage code files: git add src/                        │
   311→│     - Stage task file: git add tasks/[feature].md          │
   312→│     - Pre-commit hook verifies both staged                   │
   313→│     - Commit succeeds ✅                                     │
   314→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   315→```
   316→
   317→---
   318→
   319→## Troubleshooting
   320→
   321→### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running
   322→
   323→**Symptoms:**
   324→- Can commit code without task file
   325→- No error message appears
   326→
   327→**Causes & Fixes:**
   328→
   329→**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
   330→```bash
   331→# Fix: Make it executable
   332→chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
   333→```
   334→
   335→**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
   336→```bash
   337→# Check if file exists
   338→ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   339→
   340→# If missing, recreate from template (if you have one)
   341→# Or re-run setup script
   342→```
   343→
   344→**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
   345→```bash
   346→# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
   347→git commit  # ✅ Correct
   348→git commit --no-verify  # ❌ Bypasses hook
   349→```
   350→
   351→**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
   352→```bash
   353→# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe)
   354→# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
   355→```
   356→
   357→---
   358→
   359→### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits
   360→
   361→**Symptoms:**
   362→- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
   363→- False positive error
   364→
   365→**Causes & Fixes:**
   366→
   367→**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
   368→```bash
   369→# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
   370→git add tasks/my-feature.md  # ✅ Correct
   371→git add my-feature.md         # ❌ Wrong location
   372→git add docs/task.md          # ❌ Wrong location
   373→```
   374→
   375→**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
   376→```bash
   377→# Check what's staged
   378→git diff --cached --name-only
   379→
   380→# Ensure task file is in the list
   381→# If not, stage it:
   382→git add tasks/my-feature.md
   383→```
   384→
   385→**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
   386→```bash
   387→# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
   388→git add tasks/my-feature.md   # ✅ Correct
   389→git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # ❌ Wrong extension
   390→```
   391→
   392→---
   393→
   394→### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template
   395→
   396→**Symptoms:**
   397→- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
   398→- File not found error
   399→
   400→**Fixes:**
   401→```bash
   402→# Check if template exists
   403→ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md
   404→
   405→# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
   406→ls -la .claude/
   407→
   408→# Ensure you're in repository root
   409→pwd  # Should end with system.knowledgebase
   410→```
   411→
   412→---
   413→
   414→### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File
   415→
   416→**Symptoms:**
   417→- Code commits go through without task file
   418→- Hook seems to not detect code changes
   419→
   420→**Causes & Fixes:**
   421→
   422→**Cause 1:** Code not in src/ directory
   423→```bash
   424→# Hook only checks src/ directory
   425→# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it
   426→
   427→# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
   428→# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
   429→# Find line: CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/')
   430→# Add more patterns: grep -E '^(src/|lib/|components/)'
   431→```
   432→
   433→**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
   434→```bash
   435→# Hook ignores .md files in src/
   436→# (to allow README updates without task file)
   437→
   438→# This is intentional behavior
   439→```
   440→
   441→---
   442→
   443→### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying
   444→
   445→**Symptoms:**
   446→- Commit blocked but no error message shown
   447→- Silent failure
   448→
   449→**Fixes:**
   450→```bash
   451→# Test hook manually
   452→bash .git/hooks/pre-commit
   453→
   454→# Check for syntax errors
   455→bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit
   456→
   457→# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
   458→# Some terminals don't support color codes
   459→```
   460→
   461→---
   462→
   463→## Customization
   464→
   465→### Adding More File Patterns to Hook
   466→
   467→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)
   468→
   469→```bash
   470→# Current pattern (only src/)
   471→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')
   472→
   473→# Add more directories (lib/, config/, etc.)
   474→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
   475→```
   476→
   477→### Excluding Specific Files from Hook
   478→
   479→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)
   480→
   481→```bash
   482→# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
   483→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$)')
   484→```
   485→
   486→### Customizing Task Template
   487→
   488→**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   489→
   490→Add/remove sections as needed:
   491→- Additional checklists
   492→- Project-specific requirements
   493→- Custom metadata fields
   494→
   495→**Example additions:**
   496→```markdown
   497→## Security Checklist
   498→- [ ] Input validation added
   499→- [ ] XSS prevention verified
   500→- [ ] SQL injection prevented
   501→
   502→## Performance Checklist
   503→- [ ] Bundle size impact < 10KB
   504→- [ ] No memory leaks
   505→- [ ] Animations 60fps
   506→```
   507→
   508→### Adding New Custom Commands
   509→
   510→**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`
   511→
   512→Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`
   513→
   514→```markdown
   515→# Review Task Checklist
   516→
   517→Before marking task complete:
   518→
   519→- [ ] Review section filled out
   520→- [ ] All files documented
   521→- [ ] Testing complete
   522→- [ ] Documentation updated
   523→- [ ] Bundle size checked
   524→```
   525→
   526→### Adjusting Hook Error Messages
   527→
   528→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Lines 30-70)
   529→
   530→Customize:
   531→- Error message text
   532→- Color scheme (ANSI codes)
   533→- Instructions provided
   534→- Examples shown
   535→
   536→---
   537→
   538→## Best Practices
   539→
   540→### 1. Always Start with the Checklist
   541→
   542→Before starting any task:
   543→```bash
   544→# Read the workflow checklist
   545→cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   546→
   547→# Or use custom command
   548→/start-task  # If available in Claude Code
   549→```
   550→
   551→### 2. Create Task File FIRST
   552→
   553→```bash
   554→# ✅ CORRECT ORDER
   555→cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
   556→# Fill out the task file
   557→# THEN start coding
   558→
   559→# ❌ WRONG ORDER
   560→# Start coding first
   561→# Create task file later (will forget details!)
   562→```
   563→
   564→### 3. Update Task File as You Work
   565→
   566→```markdown
   567→## Todo Checklist
   568→
   569→### Phase 1
   570→- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE (took 30 min)
   571→- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
   572→- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for user input)
   573→```
   574→
   575→Keep the task file up to date, don't batch updates at the end.
   576→
   577→### 4. Fill Out Review Section Immediately
   578→
   579→When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
   580→- Summary of changes
   581→- Files created/modified
   582→- Testing status
   583→- Issues encountered
   584→
   585→Don't wait days/weeks - you'll forget important details!
   586→
   587→### 5. Stage Task File with Code
   588→
   589→```bash
   590→# ✅ CORRECT - Stage both together
   591→git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   592→git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
   593→git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"
   594→
   595→# ❌ WRONG - Separate commits
   596→git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   597→git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
   598→# Oops, forgot task file! (Hook will block this)
   599→```
   600→
   601→### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names
   602→
   603→```bash
   604→# ✅ GOOD - Descriptive, specific
   605→tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
   606→tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
   607→tasks/refactor-zustand-stores.md
   608→
   609→# ❌ BAD - Vague, generic
   610→tasks/todo.md
   611→tasks/task1.md
   612→tasks/stuff.md
   613→tasks/changes.md
   614→```
   615→
   616→### 7. One Task File Per Feature
   617→
   618→Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.
   619→
   620→```bash
   621→# ✅ CORRECT - Separate files
   622→tasks/add-search-feature.md
   623→tasks/add-export-feature.md
   624→
   625→# ❌ WRONG - One giant file
   626→tasks/all-features.md
   627→```
   628→
   629→### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES
   630→
   631→```bash
   632→# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
   633→git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
   634→git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
   635→git commit --no-verify  # Doc-only changes (maybe)
   636→
   637→# NOT for:
   638→git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
   639→git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later" (you won't!)
   640→```
   641→
   642→### 9. Keep Documentation Updated
   643→
   644→If architecture changes, update docs:
   645→```bash
   646→git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
   647→git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
   648→git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md  # New architecture doc
   649→git commit -m "feat: Add undo system
   650→
   651→Closes #42"
   652→```
   653→
   654→### 10. Reference CLAUDE.md Rules
   655→
   656→When in doubt, check CLAUDE.md:
   657→```bash
   658→# Quick reference
   659→cat CLAUDE.md | grep "^[0-9])"
   660→
   661→# Full read
   662→cat CLAUDE.md
   663→```
   664→
   665→---
   666→
   667→## Integration with Claude Code
   668→
   669→### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code
   670→
   671→1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
   672→2. **Use task template** for all planning
   673→3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
   674→4. **Fill review section** when done
   675→5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged
   676→
   677→### Behavioral Changes for Claude Code
   678→
   679→**Before starting ANY task:**
   680→- ✅ Read `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   681→- ✅ Create task file from template
   682→- ✅ Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)
   683→
   684→**During implementation:**
   685→- ✅ Update todos as you complete them
   686→- ✅ Document decisions in task file
   687→- ✅ Track time estimates vs actual
   688→
   689→**When complete:**
   690→- ✅ Fill out review section
   691→- ✅ Update docs/ if architecture changed
   692→- ✅ Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)
   693→
   694→**On every commit:**
   695→- ✅ Stage code files AND task file together
   696→- ✅ Git hook verifies compliance
   697→- ✅ Commit proceeds
   698→
   699→---
   700→
   701→## Appendix
   702→
   703→### File Structure Reference
   704→
   705→```
   706→system.knowledgebase/
   707→├── .git/
   708→│   └── hooks/
   709→│       └── pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
   710→├── .claude/
   711→│   ├── automation-guide.md         # This file
   712→│   ├── workflow-checklist.md       # Step-by-step checklist
   713→│   ├── templates/
   714→│   │   └── task-template.md        # Task file template
   715→│   └── commands/
   716→│       └── start-task.md           # Custom command
   717→├── tasks/
   718→│   └── [feature-name].md           # Task files (one per feature)
   719→├── CLAUDE.md                        # Main workflow guide (rules #1-11)
   720→└── README.md                        # User-facing documentation
   721→```
   722→
   723→### Git Hook Exit Codes
   724→
   725→- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
   726→- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)
   727→
   728→### ANSI Color Codes Used
   729→
   730→```bash
   731→RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
   732→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
   733→GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
   734→BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
   735→NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
   736→```
   737→
   738→---
   739→
   740→## Support & Questions
   741→
   742→**For questions about:**
   743→- **CLAUDE.md workflow:** See `CLAUDE.md` in root
   744→- **Workflow steps:** See `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   745→- **Task templates:** See `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   746→- **Git hook issues:** See troubleshooting section above
   747→- **Feature requests:** Create GitHub issue
   748→
   749→---
   750→
   751→**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
   752→**Maintained By:** Engineering Team
   753→**Version:** 1.0
   754→
   755→**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. They catch mistakes BEFORE they become problems. Follow the workflow, and your development process will be smoother and more consistent.
   756→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide

**Version:** 1.0
**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview](#overview)
2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
7. [Customization](#customization)
8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)

---

## Overview

This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md rules #1-11 by:

✅ **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
✅ **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
✅ **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
✅ **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists

**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.

---

## Quick Start

### For Developers

1. **Before starting any task:**
   ```bash
   # Check workflow checklist
   cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   ```

2. **Create task file from template:**
   ```bash
   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   ```

3. **Git hook automatically enforces:**
   - Task file must be staged when committing code changes
   - Will block commit if missing

### For Claude Code

1. **Use `/start-task` command** (if available)
2. **Reference workflow checklist** before starting
3. **Use task template** for all new tasks
4. **Git hook will catch** if task file missing

---

## Automation Tools

### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook

**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7

**How it works:**
1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)

**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  ❌ COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.

Changed code files:
  - src/components/Modal.tsx
  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts

Required Action:
  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
  3. Retry commit: git commit
```

**Emergency Bypass:**
```bash
git commit --no-verify
```
⚠️ **Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies

---

### 2. Workflow Checklist

**Location:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
**Enforces:** All rules #1-11

**When to use:**
- Before starting ANY task
- During implementation (reference as needed)
- Before marking work complete
- Before creating git commits

**Sections:**
- ✅ Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
- ✅ During Implementation
- ✅ When Complete
- ✅ Git Commit Checklist
- ✅ Rules #1-11 Quick Reference

**View checklist:**
```bash
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
```

---

### 3. Task Template

**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7

**Sections included:**
- Overview and problem statement
- Files to read
- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
- Todo checklist with time estimates
- Design considerations
- Implementation notes
- Testing checklist
- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)

**Create new task:**
```bash
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Good naming examples:**
- ✅ `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
- ✅ `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
- ✅ `tasks/notes-ux-improvements.md`

**Bad naming examples:**
- ❌ `tasks/todo.md`
- ❌ `tasks/task1.md`
- ❌ `tasks/new.md`

---

### 4. Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow reminders

#### `/start-task` Command

**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
**Purpose:** Comprehensive reminder before starting any task

**What it covers:**
1. Sync with main branch (Rule #11)
2. Read CLAUDE.md (Rules #1-11)
3. Create task file from template
4. Fill out task plan
5. Get user approval (Rule #3)
6. Implementation workflow
7. Review section and documentation updates

**Use when:**
- Starting a new task
- Unsure of the workflow
- Need a quick reference

---

## Setup Instructions

### Initial Setup (One-Time)

The automation tools are already set up in this repository. No additional installation required.

**Verify setup:**
```bash
# Check git hook exists and is executable
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check automation files exist
ls -la .claude/

# Expected output:
# .claude/
# ├── automation-guide.md         (this file)
# ├── workflow-checklist.md       (step-by-step checklist)
# ├── templates/
# │   └── task-template.md        (task file template)
# └── commands/
#     └── start-task.md           (custom command)
```

### For New Developers

1. **Clone repository**
2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (especially rules #1-11)
3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
5. **Test git hook:**
   ```bash
   # Make a small change to src/ file
   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx

   # Try to commit without staging task file
   git add src/main.tsx
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should be BLOCKED

   # Stage task file and retry
   git add tasks/some-task.md
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should succeed

   # Revert test
   git reset HEAD~1
   git checkout src/main.tsx
   ```

---

## How It Works

### Git Hook Workflow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Developer runs: git commit                                  │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Pre-commit hook executes (.git/hooks/pre-commit)           │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Check: Are any src/ files staged?                          │
│  (src/, components/, widgets/, stores/, services/)          │
└────────────┬────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┘
             │ YES                    │ NO
             ▼                        ▼
┌──────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ Check: Is tasks/*.md     │  │ Allow commit                 │
│ file staged?             │  │ (doc-only, config, etc.)     │
└──────┬───────────┬───────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
       │ YES       │ NO
       ▼           ▼
┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ✅ Allow   │  │ ❌ BLOCK commit                             │
│ commit     │  │ Display error message with instructions     │
└────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Task File Lifecycle

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. Start Task                                               │
│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
│     - Create todo checklist with time estimates             │
│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  3. Implementation                                           │
│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
│     - Add implementation notes                               │
│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  4. Completion                                               │
│     - Fill out review section                                │
│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
│     - Add testing status, time comparison                    │
│     - Update docs/ if architecture changed                   │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  5. Commit                                                   │
│     - Stage code files: git add src/                        │
│     - Stage task file: git add tasks/[feature].md          │
│     - Pre-commit hook verifies both staged                   │
│     - Commit succeeds ✅                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Troubleshooting

### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running

**Symptoms:**
- Can commit code without task file
- No error message appears

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
```bash
# Fix: Make it executable
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
```

**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
```bash
# Check if file exists
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# If missing, recreate from template (if you have one)
# Or re-run setup script
```

**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
```bash
# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
git commit  # ✅ Correct
git commit --no-verify  # ❌ Bypasses hook
```

**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
```bash
# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe)
# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
```

---

### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits

**Symptoms:**
- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
- False positive error

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
```bash
# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
git add tasks/my-feature.md  # ✅ Correct
git add my-feature.md         # ❌ Wrong location
git add docs/task.md          # ❌ Wrong location
```

**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
```bash
# Check what's staged
git diff --cached --name-only

# Ensure task file is in the list
# If not, stage it:
git add tasks/my-feature.md
```

**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
```bash
# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
git add tasks/my-feature.md   # ✅ Correct
git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # ❌ Wrong extension
```

---

### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template

**Symptoms:**
- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
- File not found error

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Check if template exists
ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md

# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
ls -la .claude/

# Ensure you're in repository root
pwd  # Should end with system.knowledgebase
```

---

### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File

**Symptoms:**
- Code commits go through without task file
- Hook seems to not detect code changes

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Code not in src/ directory
```bash
# Hook only checks src/ directory
# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it

# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
# Find line: CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/')
# Add more patterns: grep -E '^(src/|lib/|components/)'
```

**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
```bash
# Hook ignores .md files in src/
# (to allow README updates without task file)

# This is intentional behavior
```

---

### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying

**Symptoms:**
- Commit blocked but no error message shown
- Silent failure

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Test hook manually
bash .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check for syntax errors
bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
# Some terminals don't support color codes
```

---

## Customization

### Adding More File Patterns to Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)

```bash
# Current pattern (only src/)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')

# Add more directories (lib/, config/, etc.)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
```

### Excluding Specific Files from Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)

```bash
# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$)')
```

### Customizing Task Template

**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`

Add/remove sections as needed:
- Additional checklists
- Project-specific requirements
- Custom metadata fields

**Example additions:**
```markdown
## Security Checklist
- [ ] Input validation added
- [ ] XSS prevention verified
- [ ] SQL injection prevented

## Performance Checklist
- [ ] Bundle size impact < 10KB
- [ ] No memory leaks
- [ ] Animations 60fps
```

### Adding New Custom Commands

**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`

Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`

```markdown
# Review Task Checklist

Before marking task complete:

- [ ] Review section filled out
- [ ] All files documented
- [ ] Testing complete
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Bundle size checked
```

### Adjusting Hook Error Messages

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Lines 30-70)

Customize:
- Error message text
- Color scheme (ANSI codes)
- Instructions provided
- Examples shown

---

## Best Practices

### 1. Always Start with the Checklist

Before starting any task:
```bash
# Read the workflow checklist
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md

# Or use custom command
/start-task  # If available in Claude Code
```

### 2. Create Task File FIRST

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT ORDER
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
# Fill out the task file
# THEN start coding

# ❌ WRONG ORDER
# Start coding first
# Create task file later (will forget details!)
```

### 3. Update Task File as You Work

```markdown
## Todo Checklist

### Phase 1
- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE (took 30 min)
- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for user input)
```

Keep the task file up to date, don't batch updates at the end.

### 4. Fill Out Review Section Immediately

When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
- Summary of changes
- Files created/modified
- Testing status
- Issues encountered

Don't wait days/weeks - you'll forget important details!

### 5. Stage Task File with Code

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT - Stage both together
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"

# ❌ WRONG - Separate commits
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
# Oops, forgot task file! (Hook will block this)
```

### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names

```bash
# ✅ GOOD - Descriptive, specific
tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
tasks/refactor-zustand-stores.md

# ❌ BAD - Vague, generic
tasks/todo.md
tasks/task1.md
tasks/stuff.md
tasks/changes.md
```

### 7. One Task File Per Feature

Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT - Separate files
tasks/add-search-feature.md
tasks/add-export-feature.md

# ❌ WRONG - One giant file
tasks/all-features.md
```

### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES

```bash
# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
git commit --no-verify  # Doc-only changes (maybe)

# NOT for:
git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later" (you won't!)
```

### 9. Keep Documentation Updated

If architecture changes, update docs:
```bash
git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md  # New architecture doc
git commit -m "feat: Add undo system

Closes #42"
```

### 10. Reference CLAUDE.md Rules

When in doubt, check CLAUDE.md:
```bash
# Quick reference
cat CLAUDE.md | grep "^[0-9])"

# Full read
cat CLAUDE.md
```

---

## Integration with Claude Code

### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code

1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
2. **Use task template** for all planning
3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
4. **Fill review section** when done
5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged

### Behavioral Changes for Claude Code

**Before starting ANY task:**
- ✅ Read `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
- ✅ Create task file from template
- ✅ Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)

**During implementation:**
- ✅ Update todos as you complete them
- ✅ Document decisions in task file
- ✅ Track time estimates vs actual

**When complete:**
- ✅ Fill out review section
- ✅ Update docs/ if architecture changed
- ✅ Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)

**On every commit:**
- ✅ Stage code files AND task file together
- ✅ Git hook verifies compliance
- ✅ Commit proceeds

---

## Appendix

### File Structure Reference

```
system.knowledgebase/
├── .git/
│   └── hooks/
│       └── pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
├── .claude/
│   ├── automation-guide.md         # This file
│   ├── workflow-checklist.md       # Step-by-step checklist
│   ├── templates/
│   │   └── task-template.md        # Task file template
│   └── commands/
│       └── start-task.md           # Custom command
├── tasks/
│   └── [feature-name].md           # Task files (one per feature)
├── CLAUDE.md                        # Main workflow guide (rules #1-11)
└── README.md                        # User-facing documentation
```

### Git Hook Exit Codes

- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)

### ANSI Color Codes Used

```bash
RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
```

---

## Support & Questions

**For questions about:**
- **CLAUDE.md workflow:** See `CLAUDE.md` in root
- **Workflow steps:** See `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
- **Task templates:** See `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
- **Git hook issues:** See troubleshooting section above
- **Feature requests:** Create GitHub issue

---

**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
**Maintained By:** Engineering Team
**Version:** 1.0

**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. They catch mistakes BEFORE they become problems. Follow the workflow, and your development process will be smoother and more consistent.

input.content:
# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide

**Version:** 2.0
**Last Updated:** December 2025
**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools for tdr.portfolio

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview](#overview)
2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
7. [Customization](#customization)
8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)

---

## Overview

This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md rules #1-15 by:

✅ **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rules #1, #2)
✅ **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
✅ **Enforcing standards** through checklists

**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.

**Project Context:**
- Tech Stack: Pure HTML/CSS/JS (no frameworks)
- Purpose: Landing page + X automation
- No build tools, no npm, no dependencies

---

## Quick Start

### Before Starting ANY Task

1. **Sync with master branch (Rule #11):**
   ```bash
   git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
   ```

2. **Check workflow checklist:**
   ```bash
   cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   ```

3. **Create task file from template:**
   ```bash
   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   ```

4. **Get user approval (Rule #3):**
   Use `ExitPlanMode` or ask user before coding

### For Landing Page Changes

```bash
# Start local server
python3 -m http.server 8000
# Visit: http://localhost:8000
```

### For Automation Testing

```bash
# Test with dry run
DRY_RUN=true python automation/generate_post.py
```

---

## Automation Tools

### 1. Workflow Checklist

**Location:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
**Enforces:** All rules #1-15

**Sections:**
- ✅ Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
- ✅ During Implementation
- ✅ When Complete
- ✅ Git Commit Checklist
- ✅ Rules #1-15 Quick Reference

---

### 2. Task Template

**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7

**Sections included:**
- Overview and problem statement
- Files to read
- Proposed changes
- Todo checklist
- Design considerations
- Testing checklist
- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)

**Create new task:**
```bash
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Good naming examples:**
- ✅ `tasks/optimize-gif-loading.md`
- ✅ `tasks/add-new-social-link.md`
- ✅ `tasks/update-automation-prompt.md`

---

### 3. Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow

#### `/start-task` Command

**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
**Purpose:** Intelligent task router

**What it covers:**
1. Routes to appropriate workflow
2. Creates task files
3. Checks todos
4. Initiates planning

---

## Setup Instructions

### File Structure

```
tdr.portfolio/
├── .claude/
│   ├── automation-guide.md       (this file)
│   ├── workflow-checklist.md     (step-by-step checklist)
│   ├── settings.json             (Claude settings)
│   ├── settings.local.json       (local overrides)
│   ├── templates/
│   │   └── task-template.md      (task file template)
│   ├── commands/
│   │   └── start-task.md         (custom command)
│   └── skills/
│       ├── README.md             (skills overview)
│       ├── tdr-code-reviewer/
│       ├── tdr-design-system/
│       ├── tdr-feature-developer/
│       └── tdr-ux-designer/
├── tasks/                         (task files go here)
├── CLAUDE.md                      (main workflow guide)
└── README.md                      (public documentation)
```

---

## How It Works

### Task File Lifecycle

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. Start Task                                               │
│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
│     - Create todo checklist                                  │
│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  3. Implementation                                           │
│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
│     - Add implementation notes                               │
│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  4. Completion                                               │
│     - Fill out review section                                │
│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
│     - Add testing status                                     │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  5. Commit                                                   │
│     - Stage all files: git add .                            │
│     - Write descriptive commit message                       │
│     - Push to master: git push origin master                │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Troubleshooting

### Landing Page Issues

**Page not loading:**
```bash
# Check if file exists
ls -la index.html

# Start local server
python3 -m http.server 8000
```

**Styles not working:**
```bash
# Check CSS file path in index.html
# Should be: <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
```

### Automation Issues

**Dry run failing:**
```bash
# Check Python version
python3 --version

# Check script exists
ls -la automation/generate_post.py

# Run with debug
DRY_RUN=true python automation/generate_post.py --debug
```

**GitHub Actions failing:**
```bash
# Check workflow file
cat .github/workflows/post_to_x.yml

# Check secrets are set (in GitHub repo settings)
# Required: GROQ_API_KEY, TWITTER_* keys
```

---

## Customization

### Adding More Skills

1. Create folder: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/`
2. Create file: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/SKILL.md`
3. Follow existing skill format (YAML frontmatter + content)
4. Update `.claude/skills/README.md`

### Customizing Task Template

**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`

For tdr.portfolio, consider adding:
```markdown
## Browser Testing Checklist
- [ ] Chrome
- [ ] Firefox
- [ ] Safari

## Responsive Testing
- [ ] Mobile (375px)
- [ ] Tablet (768px)
- [ ] Desktop (1024px+)
```

---

## Best Practices

### 1. Always Start with Checklist

```bash
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
```

### 2. Create Task File FIRST

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
# Fill out task file
# THEN start coding

# ❌ WRONG
# Start coding first
# Create task file later
```

### 3. Screenshot Before UI Changes

For ANY landing page change:
1. Screenshot current state
2. Make ONE change
3. Screenshot after
4. Compare
5. Get user approval

### 4. Test Automation with DRY_RUN

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT
DRY_RUN=true python automation/generate_post.py
# Verify output looks good
# THEN remove DRY_RUN for real post

# ❌ WRONG
# Just run live and hope it works
```

### 5. Fill Review Section Immediately

When work is complete, fill out review section RIGHT AWAY.
Don't wait - you'll forget details!

### 6. Meaningful Commit Messages

```bash
# ✅ GOOD
git commit -m "feat: Add YouTube social link

- Added new link to social menu
- Updated stagger animation timing
- Tested on all browsers"

# ❌ BAD
git commit -m "update stuff"
```

---

## Integration with Claude Code

### Using Skills

**Available tdr.portfolio skills:**
- `tdr-code-reviewer` - Code quality and compliance
- `tdr-design-system` - UI colors, typography, components
- `tdr-feature-developer` - Architecture guidance
- `tdr-ux-designer` - UX expertise

**Invoke explicitly:**
```
Use tdr-code-reviewer to review this code
Use tdr-design-system to check this styling
```

### On Every Session

1. ✅ Sync with master
2. ✅ Reference workflow checklist
3. ✅ Create/update task file
4. ✅ Get user approval before coding
5. ✅ Fill review section when done
6. ✅ Commit and push

---

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete workflow rules (#1-15)
- `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Step-by-step checklist
- `.claude/templates/task-template.md` - Task file template
- `.claude/skills/README.md` - Available skills
- `docs/automation/` - X automation guides

---

**Last Updated:** December 2025
**Project:** tdr.portfolio (thedeadrobot.com)

**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. Follow the workflow for smoother, more consistent development.

# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide

**Version:** 2.0
**Last Updated:** December 2025
**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools for tdr.portfolio

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview](#overview)
2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
7. [Customization](#customization)
8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)

---

## Overview

This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md rules #1-15 by:

✅ **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rules #1, #2)
✅ **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
✅ **Enforcing standards** through checklists

**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.

**Project Context:**
- Tech Stack: Pure HTML/CSS/JS (no frameworks)
- Purpose: Landing page + X automation
- No build tools, no npm, no dependencies

---

## Quick Start

### Before Starting ANY Task

1. **Sync with master branch (Rule #11):**
   ```bash
   git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
   ```

2. **Check workflow checklist:**
   ```bash
   cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   ```

3. **Create task file from template:**
   ```bash
   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   ```

4. **Get user approval (Rule #3):**
   Use `ExitPlanMode` or ask user before coding

### For Landing Page Changes

```bash
# Start local server
python3 -m http.server 8000
# Visit: http://localhost:8000
```

### For Automation Testing

```bash
# Test with dry run
DRY_RUN=true python automation/generate_post.py
```

---

## Automation Tools

### 1. Workflow Checklist

**Location:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
**Enforces:** All rules #1-15

**Sections:**
- ✅ Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
- ✅ During Implementation
- ✅ When Complete
- ✅ Git Commit Checklist
- ✅ Rules #1-15 Quick Reference

---

### 2. Task Template

**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7

**Sections included:**
- Overview and problem statement
- Files to read
- Proposed changes
- Todo checklist
- Design considerations
- Testing checklist
- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)

**Create new task:**
```bash
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Good naming examples:**
- ✅ `tasks/optimize-gif-loading.md`
- ✅ `tasks/add-new-social-link.md`
- ✅ `tasks/update-automation-prompt.md`

---

### 3. Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow

#### `/start-task` Command

**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
**Purpose:** Intelligent task router

**What it covers:**
1. Routes to appropriate workflow
2. Creates task files
3. Checks todos
4. Initiates planning

---

## Setup Instructions

### File Structure

```
tdr.portfolio/
├── .claude/
│   ├── automation-guide.md       (this file)
│   ├── workflow-checklist.md     (step-by-step checklist)
│   ├── settings.json             (Claude settings)
│   ├── settings.local.json       (local overrides)
│   ├── templates/
│   │   └── task-template.md      (task file template)
│   ├── commands/
│   │   └── start-task.md         (custom command)
│   └── skills/
│       ├── README.md             (skills overview)
│       ├── tdr-code-reviewer/
│       ├── tdr-design-system/
│       ├── tdr-feature-developer/
│       └── tdr-ux-designer/
├── tasks/                         (task files go here)
├── CLAUDE.md                      (main workflow guide)
└── README.md                      (public documentation)
```

---

## How It Works

### Task File Lifecycle

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. Start Task                                               │
│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
│     - Create todo checklist                                  │
│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  3. Implementation                                           │
│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
│     - Add implementation notes                               │
│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  4. Completion                                               │
│     - Fill out review section                                │
│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
│     - Add testing status                                     │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  5. Commit                                                   │
│     - Stage all files: git add .                            │
│     - Write descriptive commit message                       │
│     - Push to master: git push origin master                │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Troubleshooting

### Landing Page Issues

**Page not loading:**
```bash
# Check if file exists
ls -la index.html

# Start local server
python3 -m http.server 8000
```

**Styles not working:**
```bash
# Check CSS file path in index.html
# Should be: <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
```

### Automation Issues

**Dry run failing:**
```bash
# Check Python version
python3 --version

# Check script exists
ls -la automation/generate_post.py

# Run with debug
DRY_RUN=true python automation/generate_post.py --debug
```

**GitHub Actions failing:**
```bash
# Check workflow file
cat .github/workflows/post_to_x.yml

# Check secrets are set (in GitHub repo settings)
# Required: GROQ_API_KEY, TWITTER_* keys
```

---

## Customization

### Adding More Skills

1. Create folder: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/`
2. Create file: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/SKILL.md`
3. Follow existing skill format (YAML frontmatter + content)
4. Update `.claude/skills/README.md`

### Customizing Task Template

**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`

For tdr.portfolio, consider adding:
```markdown
## Browser Testing Checklist
- [ ] Chrome
- [ ] Firefox
- [ ] Safari

## Responsive Testing
- [ ] Mobile (375px)
- [ ] Tablet (768px)
- [ ] Desktop (1024px+)
```

---

## Best Practices

### 1. Always Start with Checklist

```bash
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
```

### 2. Create Task File FIRST

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
# Fill out task file
# THEN start coding

# ❌ WRONG
# Start coding first
# Create task file later
```

### 3. Screenshot Before UI Changes

For ANY landing page change:
1. Screenshot current state
2. Make ONE change
3. Screenshot after
4. Compare
5. Get user approval

### 4. Test Automation with DRY_RUN

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT
DRY_RUN=true python automation/generate_post.py
# Verify output looks good
# THEN remove DRY_RUN for real post

# ❌ WRONG
# Just run live and hope it works
```

### 5. Fill Review Section Immediately

When work is complete, fill out review section RIGHT AWAY.
Don't wait - you'll forget details!

### 6. Meaningful Commit Messages

```bash
# ✅ GOOD
git commit -m "feat: Add YouTube social link

- Added new link to social menu
- Updated stagger animation timing
- Tested on all browsers"

# ❌ BAD
git commit -m "update stuff"
```

---

## Integration with Claude Code

### Using Skills

**Available tdr.portfolio skills:**
- `tdr-code-reviewer` - Code quality and compliance
- `tdr-design-system` - UI colors, typography, components
- `tdr-feature-developer` - Architecture guidance
- `tdr-ux-designer` - UX expertise

**Invoke explicitly:**
```
Use tdr-code-reviewer to review this code
Use tdr-design-system to check this styling
```

### On Every Session

1. ✅ Sync with master
2. ✅ Reference workflow checklist
3. ✅ Create/update task file
4. ✅ Get user approval before coding
5. ✅ Fill review section when done
6. ✅ Commit and push

---

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete workflow rules (#1-15)
- `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Step-by-step checklist
- `.claude/templates/task-template.md` - Task file template
- `.claude/skills/README.md` - Available skills
- `docs/automation/` - X automation guides

---

**Last Updated:** December 2025
**Project:** tdr.portfolio (thedeadrobot.com)

**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. Follow the workflow for smoother, more consistent development.
